text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Increased egg consumption is not associated with cardiovascular disease risk in the general population.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A 2020 umbrella review concluded that increased egg consumption is not associated with cardiovascular disease risk in the general population.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states that a review found no association between higher egg intake and cardiovascular disease risk for the general population.","population":"general population","intervention":"increased egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"increased egg consumption","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg consumption of more than once daily increased cardiovascular disease risk 1.69-fold in people with type 2 diabetes mellitus compared with type 2 diabetics who ate less than one egg per week.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"but did find egg consumption of more than once daily increased cardiovascular disease risk 1.69-fold in those with type 2 diabetes mellitus when compared to type 2 diabetics who ate less than one egg per week.","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports a specific increased risk (1.69‑fold) for high egg consumption versus low consumption among individuals with type 2 diabetes.","population":"people with type 2 diabetes mellitus","intervention":"egg consumption of more than once daily","comparator":"type 2 diabetics who ate less than one egg per week","outcome":"cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"more than once daily; 1.69‑fold","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Eating four eggs per week increased the risk of cardiovascular disease by six percent.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Another 2013 meta-analysis found that eating four eggs per week increased the risk of cardiovascular disease by six percent.","entailment":"The quoted meta‑analysis result directly links a weekly intake of four eggs to a 6% higher cardiovascular disease risk.","population":"","intervention":"eating four eggs per week","comparator":"","outcome":"cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"four eggs per week; six percent","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Eggs are one of the largest sources of phosphatidylcholine (lecithin) in the human diet.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Eggs are one of the largest sources of phosphatidylcholine ( lecithin) in the human diet.","entailment":"The sentence defines eggs as a major dietary source of phosphatidylcholine/lecithin.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Dietary phosphatidylcholine is digested by gut bacteria and converted into TMAO, a compound linked with increased heart disease.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"showed that dietary phosphatidylcholine is digested by bacteria in the gut and eventually converted into the compound TMAO, a compound linked with increased heart disease.","entailment":"The quoted study reports the metabolic pathway from phosphatidylcholine to TMAO and notes its association with higher heart disease risk.","population":"","intervention":"dietary phosphatidylcholine","comparator":"","outcome":"increased heart disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg consumption does not increase hypertension risk.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Egg consumption does not increase hypertension risk.","entailment":"The sentence explicitly states that there is no observed increase in hypertension risk from eating eggs.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"hypertension risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Consumption of up to one egg a day may contribute to a decreased risk of total stroke.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A 2016 meta-analysis found that consumption of up to one egg a day may contribute to a decreased risk of total stroke.","entailment":"The quoted meta‑analysis suggests a possible protective effect of up to one egg per day on total stroke risk.","population":"","intervention":"consumption of up to one egg a day","comparator":"","outcome":"total stroke risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"up to one egg a day","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg consumption has no significant effect on serum biomarkers of inflammation.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A 2019 meta-analysis revealed that egg consumption has no significant effect on serum biomarkers of inflammation.","entailment":"The quoted meta‑analysis reports a null finding regarding the impact of egg intake on inflammatory biomarkers.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"serum biomarkers of inflammation","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg consumption has beneficial effects on macular pigment optical density and serum lutein.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A 2021 review of clinical trials found that egg consumption has beneficial effects on macular pigment optical density and serum lutein.","entailment":"The quoted review states that eating eggs improves macular pigment density and increases serum lutein levels.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"macular pigment optical density and serum lutein","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Only 2.3 million eggs are contaminated with Salmonella, about one in every 30,000 eggs, indicating Salmonella infection from eggs is rare.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"only 2.3 million are contaminated with Salmonella—equivalent to just one in every 30,000 eggs—thus showing Salmonella infection is quite rarely induced by eggs.","entailment":"The quoted statistic quantifies the low contamination rate, supporting the claim that egg‑related Salmonella infection is uncommon.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"Salmonella contamination rate","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"2.3 million; one in every 30,000 eggs","modality":"descriptive"}]}
